mode_description.off: AI stays silent. Threshold and manual incidents still open, but no analysis is attached.
mode_description.suggest: AI writes analysis on every incident and raises suggestions for anomalies. You decide what to promote or act on.
mode_description.auto: AI opens anomaly incidents on its own, drives every incident (threshold, anomaly, manual) and auto-resolves when healthy.

errors.generic_update: Could not save AI settings. Please try again.

default_mode.title: Default mode
default_mode.subtitle: New monitors inherit this mode. Each monitor may override.

behavior.title: Behavior
behavior.subtitle: Fine-tune what the AI is allowed to do.
behavior.auto_resolve_subtitle: Close incidents automatically after 5 healthy checks in a row.
behavior.digest_subtitle: AI writes a short weekly recap of incidents and metric trends every Monday morning.
behavior.similarity_subtitle: Use the 90-day incident history to suggest likely causes and fixes.

preview.title: Preview
preview.subtitle: How the AI will communicate under this mode.
preview.sample_off: AI is off. Uptizm will detect incidents but no analysis, summary, or suggestions will be generated.
preview.sample_suggest: db.conn_ms crossed the warn band 3 checks in a row. Matches a pattern from 2 prior pool-exhaustion incidents. Would you like me to open an incident and propose actions?
preview.sample_auto: Opened incident: db.conn_ms degraded. Suggested action queued: raise pool size to 40. Watching next 5 checks; will auto-resolve if it recovers.

gating.title: When does AI run?
gating.subtitle: The checks AI performs before spending tokens on this monitor.

ok.title: Armed and ready
ok.hint: When status or consecutive failures change, AI evaluates the check.

mode_off.title: Mode is off
mode_off.hint: If effective mode is Off, AI never runs. Enable Suggest or Auto here or on a monitor.

below_fail_threshold.title: Single-blip filter
below_fail_threshold.hint: A single failed check is ignored. AI waits until failures reach the incident threshold to avoid false alarms.

active_ai_incident.title: One AI incident at a time
active_ai_incident.hint: While an AI-owned incident is open, further runs are paused so we don't pile on suggestions.

state_unchanged.title: Nothing changed
state_unchanged.hint: If status and consecutive failures match the previous run, we skip — no new signal to evaluate.

cooldown.title: Cooldown window
cooldown.hint: A short cool-down (3x check interval, capped between 2 and 15 minutes) spaces out runs so bursts don't burn budget.

coming_soon.title: Coming soon
coming_soon.subtitle: This area is on the roadmap. We will ship it shortly.

basics.subtitle: Name the monitor and tell us what to probe.

alerts.title: Alerts
alerts.subtitle: We will notify you when something goes wrong.

auth.title: Authentication
auth.subtitle: Send credentials with every check if your endpoint is protected.
auth.security_note: Credentials are encrypted at rest and never shown in logs or check results.

advanced.title: Advanced
advanced.subtitle: Fine-tune the request (HTTP only).
advanced.expected_status_hint: The check passes when the response code matches.
advanced.timeout_hint: Fail the check if the response takes longer.
advanced.headers_hint: Sent with every request. Useful for auth tokens or API keys.

welcome.title: Monitor is live; add your first custom metric
welcome.subtitle: Uptime alone only tells half the story. Extract a value from the response to track latency, queue depth, cache hit rate, anything.

fields.name: Monitor name

fields.type_hint: Pick the protocol that matches your endpoint.
fields.alert_down_subtitle: Open an incident the moment a check fails.
fields.alert_warn_subtitle: Also notify on degraded responses (threshold band: warn).

empty.stats_subtitle: Stats will populate after the first few checks.
empty.performance_subtitle: Performance samples appear once checks start running.
empty.uptime_subtitle: We need at least a day of checks to draw the timeline.
empty.checks_subtitle: Recent checks will appear here as they run.

metric_form.subtitle: Extract a value from the response and turn it into a trackable metric.

basics.title: Basics
basics.subtitle: A human label, an internal key, and a group to file it under.

parse.title: How to extract
parse.subtitle: Pick a parser and tell us where to read the value from.

type_section.title: Value type

threshold.title: Thresholds

preview_section.title: Preview
preview_section.subtitle: A quick look at what this metric will render.

fields.label: Display label
fields.label_hint: Shown in the dashboard (e.g. DB Size).
fields.key_hint: Stable id used in exports, queries and the API.
fields.group_hint: Pick an existing group or type a new one.
fields.group_empty: No matches. Press enter to create.
fields.source_hint: How we should locate the value in the response.
fields.path_hint: Expression fed to the selected parser.
fields.fallback_hint: Value to use if the parser finds nothing.
fields.unit_hint: Displayed next to the value (ms, MB, %, ...).
fields.unit_kind_hint: How the raw number is scaled and labeled.

errors.path_required: Path / pattern is required for this parser.

key_validation.too_long: Key must be 40 characters or fewer.
key_validation.format: Use lowercase letters, digits and underscores only (must start with a letter).

direction_validation.high_bad: Warn must be lower than Critical when higher values are worse.
direction_validation.low_bad: Warn must be higher than Critical when lower values are worse.

metric_form.direction_subtitle: Is higher worse, or is lower worse?

direction_hint.highBad: Alerts fire when the value climbs above the bands.
direction_hint.lowBad: Alerts fire when the value falls below the bands.
